skill.name: learn
skill.description: Extract project-specific insights from the current conversation and save them to a local knowledge base.
skill.summary: Save reusable project learnings from a conversation into local knowledge files.
skill.author: 0xrdan

security_audit.summary: Static shell execution, base64, weak crypto, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by markdown examples and prose. The confirmed risk is intentional filesystem persistence of conversation-derived knowledge, which could store sensitive project or user information if used without review.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Filesystem Writes Can Store Sensitive Conversation Data
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally extracts insights from the current conversation and appends them to files under knowledge/learnings, then updates knowledge/state.json. This is a legitimate feature, but it can persist secrets, confidential decisions, or sensitive project details if the conversation contains them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions directly require appending conversation-derived insights and updating state files. The risk is contextual data persistence, not malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby Shell Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are markdown list items, fenced markdown examples, or plain file names. No Ruby code or shell backtick execution is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is markdown instructions and the flagged syntax appears inside markdown formatting examples. I found no executable script content or command invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Base64 Decode Mention
low_findings.description: The base64 reference appears only inside an example quirk explaining a hypothetical token behavior and recommending a named helper. It does not decode data or execute obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The base64 text is inside a fenced markdown example. There is no executable decoding routine or hidden payload.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Indicators
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are caused by prose and markdown template words such as description, Pattern, Quirk, and Decision. No cryptographic algorithm is implemented or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are labels and frontmatter text, not cryptographic code. I did not find evidence of MD5, DES, SHA1, or similar weak algorithm use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance location is a guidance note telling the assistant not to extract obvious or trivial information. It does not instruct environment discovery, host profiling, or data collection beyond the intended learning files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a content-quality rule for knowledge extraction. No system probing command, filesystem enumeration, or network discovery instruction is present.

content.value_statement: Important project lessons often disappear after a coding session ends. This skill extracts reusable insights from the current conversation and records them in local knowledge files.
actual_capabilities: Reviews the current conversation for reusable project-specific insights.
Categorizes each insight as a pattern, quirk, or decision.
Appends formatted entries to knowledge/learnings files.
Updates knowledge state metadata after extraction.
Reports a concise summary of captured learnings to the user.
limitations: It depends on the current conversation context and may miss older details.
It can persist sensitive information if the user includes it in the conversation.
It does not validate whether every extracted insight remains true over time.
It is intended for local project knowledge, not general documentation publishing.

use_cases.title: Preserve Debugging Lessons
use_cases.description: Capture unusual project behavior discovered during a debugging session so future assistants can avoid repeated investigation.

use_cases.title: Record Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: Save implementation choices and rationale after design discussions, migrations, or library selection work.

use_cases.title: Maintain Assistant Context
use_cases.description: Build a lightweight local memory that helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code follow project-specific patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Save Basic Learning
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt after a useful session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the learn skill to capture any project-specific patterns, quirks, or decisions from this conversation.

prompt_templates.title: Capture Debugging Insight
prompt_templates.scenario: After fixing an issue with unusual behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the debugging lesson from this conversation. Save only project-specific quirks and the workaround we verified.

prompt_templates.title: Record Design Decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Use learn to record the architecture decision we made, including the context, choice, rationale, and confidence level.

prompt_templates.title: Curate Session Knowledge
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this conversation and save only high or medium confidence learnings. Exclude generic advice, secrets, temporary notes, and duplicated insights.

output_examples.input: We discovered that this project requires a custom token helper for auth tests.
output_examples.output: The skill would record a quirk with the affected area, observed behavior, workaround, discovery date, and confidence level.

output_examples.input: We decided to keep the existing data access layer instead of adding a new ORM.
output_examples.output: The skill would record a decision with the context, selected approach, rationale, and confidence level.

output_examples.input: We found that running focused tests before the full suite saves time in this repository.
output_examples.output: The skill would record a pattern that explains the workflow and why it works for this project.

best_practices: Review extracted learnings before committing knowledge files.
Capture only project-specific insights that future sessions can reuse.
Use medium confidence when an insight is reasonable but not fully verified.
anti_patterns: Do not store credentials, private customer data, or sensitive incident details.
Do not save generic programming advice that is not specific to the project.
Do not duplicate existing learnings with slightly different wording.

faq.question: What does this skill save?
faq.answer: It saves project-specific patterns, quirks, and decisions extracted from the current conversation.

faq.question: Where does it store learnings?
faq.answer: It stores entries in local knowledge/learnings files and updates knowledge/state.json.

faq.question: Does it run external commands?
faq.answer: No evidence of external command execution was found in the skill instructions.

faq.question: Can it expose secrets?
faq.answer: It can persist sensitive text if secrets appear in the conversation, so review entries before committing them.

faq.question: Is it useful for team documentation?
faq.answer: Yes, it can preserve concise project lessons, but it is not a full documentation generator.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
